course: General
difficulty: 7
est_minutes: 60
summary: This presentation introduces causal inference through practical examples,
  demonstrating how to draw causal graphs, identify direct effects, and consider confounders
  in scenarios like group efficacy, blog topic posting, and price-demand relationships,
  concluding with exercises on evaluating causal effect estimation in real-world situations.
tags:
- Causal Inference
- Directed Acyclic Graphs (DAGs)
- Confounding
- Causal Effect Estimation
- Data Science Applications
type: Lecture
---

# Some examples

Master in Data Science Upf - CI & ML - Part I

---

# Group Efficacy

---

# Example 1 - Group Efficacy

*   A company has different groups
*   Each group works in different projects
*   The company wants to know which is the efficacy (measured in hours) of each group
